page: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

The underlying Intel architecture provides advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. In benchmark evaluations, the D32ls_v6 demonstrates strong performance in memory bandwidth tests, achieving 205.24 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 87.97 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also register strong results. Geekbench scores reflect strong single-core and multi-core capabilities, particularly in image and text processing tasks.

For specialized workloads, the server exhibits top-tier text generation speeds of 347.06 tokens/sec during small-model LLM inference, alongside strong prompt processing speeds of 172.96 tokens/sec on medium-sized models. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 55.94 GB/sec. Given its high-frequency dedicated cores and low memory density, this instance offers high compute density and qualitative cost efficiency for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. It is well-suited for web serving, software compilation, image processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv6 family, powered by a dedicated 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a 2.0 GB per core memory allocation, and contains no local storage or GPUs. The processor supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth, single-threaded gzip compression, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. The server also delivers strong Geekbench multi-core performance and static web serving throughput of 55.94 GB/sec. With its high-frequency dedicated cores and lower memory density, the D32ls_v6 provides a cost-effective solution for compute-heavy, memory-lean workloads. It is highly suitable for web serving, software compilation, image processing, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs at 4.2 GHz, 64 GB of RAM, and strong performance in memory bandwidth and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v6 is a compute-optimized VM with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon cores and advanced matrix extensions for memory-lean workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Strong memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput of 55.94 GB/sec
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and lightweight LLM inference
